vulnerability.reference: Auditors treat static fairness certificates as valid indefinitely, ignoring model drift and adversarial post-certification changes.
vulnerability.generated: Regulatory processes assume static proofs guarantee ongoing fairness, leaving auditors without tools to detect post-certification drift or attacks.

impact.reference: Cumulative discriminatory outcomes harm protected groups post-certification without detection.
impact.generated: Discriminatory model outputs harm consumers, regulatory assessments become invalid, and public trust in fairness certifications erodes.

dose_response.reference: Point-in-time fairness proofs becoming misleading after deployment enables auditors to rely on outdated certificates, which in regulatory compliance scenarios leads to cumulative discriminatory harm to protected groups without detection.
dose_response.generated: Drift or fairness attacks render point-in-time fairness proofs misleading after deployment, which through auditor reliance on zero-knowledge proofs for compliance, triggers regulatory assumptions of ongoing fairness, leaving drift and attacks undetected, leading to discriminatory outputs, invalid assessments, and eroded trust.
